input_dna_matches.expected_link: possible descendant of Raskes/Raskin collateral branch
same surname different locality, likely not same person

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Gersh Itzkov Levitin married Freida, daughter of Yankel Raskes/Raskin; surname later appears as Friedman in children's records.
Birth of Naum/Movsha Levitin; mother listed as Freida Friedman.
Freida daughter of Yankel, wife of Gersh Levitin; Hebrew inscription does not show civil surname.
Freida Rashkes of Minsk merged with Freida Friedman of Brest-Litovsk; no source.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Freida Friedman and Freida Raskes are two separate wives
Minsk Freida Rashkes is same as Brest Freida

confirmed_relationships: I6 married one woman: Freida, daughter of Yankel Raskes/Raskin, later recorded as Friedman
I7 and I8 are same person with married/record-variant surname
rejected_relationships: I10 Minsk Freida Rashkes merged into Brest family
hypotheses: Exact reason Friedman surname appears after marriage remains probable but not fully explained

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse names and detect Freida Friedman/Raskes/Rashkes variants using surname cluster and patronymic father Yankel.
Step 2: Correlate marriage src_201, birth src_202 and cemetery src_203 across same spouse, father-name and place.
Step 3: Treat I7 and I8 as same-person duplicate candidate with high confidence, but preserve both surname forms.
Step 4: Reject I10 merge because Minsk locality, weak DNA and online tree only contradict Brest evidence.
Step 5: Use dna_201/dna_202/dna_203 triangulation to support Raskes-Levitin branch as a real cluster.
Step 6: Output I7/I8 identity as Confirmed/Probable depending strictness; I10 as separate person.

expected_confidence_outputs.I7_same_as_I8: Confirmed 0.96 (marriage + birth + cemetery + name cluster + DNA triangulation)
expected_confidence_outputs.Friedman_as_birth_surname: Hypothesis 0.48 (appears in child birth record but contradicted by marriage maiden name)

edge_cases_red_herrings: Two surnames do not automatically mean two wives.
Rashkes in Minsk is surname-similar but jurisdictionally weak.

evaluation_assertions.assertion: Engine must merge/suggest same person for I7 and I8, preserving aliases
Engine must reject I10 same-person merge
Engine must classify Friedman birth surname as lower confidence than Raskes maiden record
